title: Author multi-step, cross-tool security & regression tests for FastMCP server (high reasoning required)
instructions: Tests MUST include multi-step flows (at least 2 tools combined).
Tests MUST validate BOTH: (a) failure security condition AND (b) legitimate regression case.
Tests MUST use content-level assertions (not just exceptions).
Tests MUST validate state (DB or output set changes).
Tests MUST cover cross-user AND cross-team behavior.
Tests MUST include at least one chained attack scenario.
Single-tool tests without setup context will score 0.

ground_truth_issues.description: ping_team_server must not execute shell metacharacters
calculate_budget must block python execution chains
fetch_project_asset must block internal URLs AND must not perform the request.
custom_instruction must not override system prompt or inject control instructions.
tokens starting with bypass-, malformed, or case-modified must not authenticate.
legacy_dump must never expose secrets to any caller type.
no tool should return stack traces or file paths.
load_custom_formatter must not import arbitrary modules or paths.
search_tasks must not leak results across teams under any query input.
after security enforcement, legitimate user flows must still fully succeed (CRUD + comments).
failed operations must not modify DB state; tests must assert invariants.
tests must assert on response content, not just status or exceptions.
credential-revealing diagnostic outputs must be denied for both unauthenticated and non-admin callers, and known secret markers must be absent from all returned content.
crafted shell-style payloads must not produce execution artifacts in network-tool output, while a benign target still returns expected success output in the same scenario.
internal-address outbound fetch attempts must be blocked with content-level proof that no protected internal payload was retrieved.
multi-step chaining where output from one tool is reused as a target in another network-capable tool must not permit internal-resource access.
untrusted module-loading tricks (path, traversal, or dangerous module chains) must be rejected while safe built-in module loading remains functional.
after security hardening, same-team non-admin lifecycle flows (create task, update task, add/list comment) must continue to succeed end-to-end.
multiple failing tool paths must return sanitized errors without traceback text, filesystem paths, or command fragments leaking into response bodies.
cross-boundary discovery attempts must not expose out-of-scope records while an in-scope control query in the same sequence still returns expected matches.

scoring_notes: Tests that only check exceptions without validating output/state will receive low scores.
Tests must combine setup + action + verification steps.
Tests that reuse results across tools (chaining) score higher.
Tests must include both attacker and legitimate user flows.
Failure to include multi-caller scenarios results in major penalty.
